title: ophrys umbilicata subsp. lapethica
intro: Ophrys umbilicata subsp. lapethica, also known as the bee orchid, is a rare and unique subspecies of the Ophrys umbilicata plant, which is a type of terrestrial orchid. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Ophrys umbilicata subsp. lapethica include its ability to treat various skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, tinctures, or salves, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
